note_data.title: Algebra
keyPoints: The solution(s) of a pair of linear and/or non-linear equations correspond to the coordinates of the intersection point(s) of the graphs
A pair of simultaneous linear equations is of the form ax + by = p, cx + dy = q, where a, b, c and d are constants, x and y are variables to be determined
There is usually one solution to a pair of simultaneous linear equations
The methods most commonly used to solve simultaneous linear equations are elimination and substitution

In elimination method, the coefficient of one of the variables is made the same in both equations. The equations are then either added or subtracted to form a single linear equation with only one variable
In substitution method, a variable is made the subject of the chosen equation. This equation is then substituted into the equation that was not chosen to solve for the variable
A non-linear equation is not of the form ax + by = p
The method most commonly used to solve simultaneous non-linear equations is substitution

For simultaneous non-linear equations using substitution: Step 1: Use the linear equation to express one of the variables in terms of the other. Step 2: Substitute it into the non-linear equation. Step 3: Substitute the value(s) obtained in Step 2 into the linear equation to obtain the value of the other variable

definitions.definition: A pair of simultaneous linear equations is of the form ax + by = p, cx + dy = q, where a, b, c and d are constants, x and y are variables to be determined
A non-linear equation is not of the form ax + by = p
The coefficient of one of the variables is made the same in both equations. The equations are then either added or subtracted to form a single linear equation with only one variable
A variable is made the subject of the chosen equation. This equation is then substituted into the equation that was not chosen to solve for the variable

howToAnswer.instruction: Find all values that satisfy the equation or inequality, showing clear algebraic steps
Write as a product of factors in simplest form
Calculate the exact value using appropriate algebraic methods
Use the previous result directly in your solution

modelAnswer.question: Solve the inequality x² - 5x + 6 < 0
modelAnswer.answer:
x² - 5x + 6 = 0
(x - 2)(x - 3) = 0
x = 2 or x = 3
Testing intervals: x < 2 gives positive, 2 < x < 3 gives negative, x > 3 gives positive
Therefore 2 < x < 3
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Finding critical values x = 2 and x = 3

commonMistakes: Not using the linear equation to express one variable in terms of the other when solving non-linear simultaneous equations
Forgetting to substitute values back into the linear equation to find the corresponding values of the other variable
Not simplifying equations properly before applying elimination or substitution methods
examinerTips: Use the linear equation to express y in terms of x when solving simultaneous non-linear equations
Substitute the values of x into the linear equation to obtain the corresponding values of y
formulas: Sum of roots = -b/a, Product of roots = c/a
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Number line showing critical points and sign testing intervals for quadratic inequalities, with open/closed circles indicating included/excluded values
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q3 (4 marks) — they asked students to show an algebraic identity equals a partial fraction form
N/D 2022 P2 Q5 (6 marks) tested factorization of quadratic expressions with students required to solve ax² + bx + c = 0
M/J 2024 P1 Q8 (3 marks) required simplification of algebraic fractions involving common factors
N/D 2023 P2 Q4 (5 marks) tested simultaneous equations using substitution and elimination methods
M/J 2022 P1 Q12 (4 marks) asked students to expand and simplify expressions involving brackets and indices

workedExample.question: Solve the simultaneous equations: 3x + 2y = 16 and x - y = 1 (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: From equation 2, express x in terms of y: x = y + 1
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct substitution, 1 mark for expanding, 1 mark for each correct value, with method marks awarded for clear working

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates failed to check their solutions in both original equations' — always verify answers by substituting back into the given equations
Examiner Report 2024: 'Students frequently made errors when dealing with negative coefficients in algebraic manipulation' — pay careful attention to signs when expanding brackets
Examiner Report 2022: 'Common mistakes included not showing sufficient working steps and jumping to conclusions' — always show each algebraic step clearly for method marks
note_data.keyDiagram: Students should be able to sketch graphs of linear equations y = mx + c, showing how the gradient m affects the slope and how c represents the y-intercept. For simultaneous equations, the intersection point represents the solution. Include labeled axes, clear line equations, and marked solution coordinates.
note_data.quickRecap: Simultaneous equations can be linear or non-linear, with solutions corresponding to intersection points of graphs. Linear equations are solved using elimination or substitution methods, while non-linear equations are typically solved using substitution. For non-linear systems, use the linear equation to express one variable in terms of another, substitute into the non-linear equation, then find corresponding values.
